purpose: Determine whether the exact best calibration calendar in the complete at-most-three-switch W24 family lies on the three-switch boundary.
scientific_scope: Comparator development on 60 already-burned calibration tapes only. No locked or virgin tape may be opened. This gate cannot estimate H_PI or H_obs, close M/T/R, authorize W24, or support a Paper 2 result.

candidate_family.definition: Every feasible 24-week active M/T/R calendar beginning in M, obeying the no-adjacent-switch rule, and containing at most three active-action switches.
candidate_family.enumeration_order: Stable lexicographic depth-first action order M,T,R, pruned immediately when a fourth switch would be required.

calibration.primary_selection: maximum exact sum of the 60 emitted binary64 ret_excel_visible_v1 values; arithmetic mean is display-only
calibration.tie_break: minimum candidate-family index among exact primary ties

selected_calendar_ledger.resource_interpretation: All candidates have the same 4032 response-team hours and reserve access rules. Realized reserve issue and replenishment remain policy-dependent outcomes and are reported, not declared equal.

decision_rules.boundary_active: If the exact selected calendar contains exactly three switches, report CALIBRATION_SWITCH_COMPLEXITY_BOUNDARY_ACTIVE. This authorizes only a separately frozen richer comparator-development gate.
decision_rules.interior: If the exact selected calendar contains fewer than three switches, report CALIBRATION_SWITCH_COMPLEXITY_INTERIOR. This stops the switch-complexity sieve but does not prove that calendars with more switches cannot win.
